answeredQuestions.initiative-direction: Create a new PRD that replaces the separate-mode direction with an agentic-first redesign.
answeredQuestions.architecture-strategy: Rework the current RAG-focused code to support the desired agentic curation workflow while reusing durable plumbing.
answeredQuestions.primary-reference: The primary UX goal is defined in wireframes/agent-curation-wireframe-v2.2.html.
answeredQuestions.schema-source: The generic schema is defined in wireframes/gt_schema_v5_generic.py.
answeredQuestions.rag-compatibility-strategy: The existing RAG-focused curation flow must run in the redesigned architecture via UI plugins and extension points.
answeredQuestions.legacy-code-strategy: A lot of the current code should be deleted and replaced because there are no current users of the existing setup.
answeredQuestions.q-001-minimal-plugin-surface: The minimal RAG compatibility surface must include a retrieval review plugin experience that can bind search backends, let curators select primary and bonus references, persist those selections in plugin-owned data on the generic core, and integrate with validation, explorer, computed-tag, and import/export extension points without restoring RAG-only core fields.
answeredQuestions.q-002-legacy-payload-adaptation: Legacy RAG payloads should be adapted at import/export boundaries only. The redesigned system should store and read the normalized generic record, and any migration toggles used during implementation must remain temporary rather than becoming a shipped transitional read path.
answeredQuestions.q-003-v1-rag-compatibility-boundary: v1 compatibility should preserve the curator-visible essentials of the current RAG flow: retrieval/search, primary and bonus reference selection, reference visit validation when enabled, multi-turn reference attachment where applicable, assignment/approval/export lifecycle continuity, and tags. Richer legacy RAG-only affordances such as stricter key-paragraph expectations, older grounding-summary/evaluation-specific surfaces, and similar non-essential conveniences may be simplified while there are no active users.
answeredQuestions.q-004-rules-engine-separation: The optional rules engine should remain fully separate in v1. The plugin model should not reserve a dedicated standard hook for rules-engine integration until the generic plugin model and the RAG compatibility pack are proven in practice.
answeredQuestions.decision-retrieval-tool-call-specializat: RAG retrieval should be modeled as a specialized extension of the generic tool-call review surface. The retrieval step remains a tool call, and the RAG plugin adds retrieval-specific affordances there for selecting primary and bonus references.
answeredQuestions.decision-action-capable-review-component: The extensibility contract should explicitly support action-capable plugin review components for tool responses and plugin-owned payloads, not just passive renderers, so the RAG plugin can select and persist primary and bonus references from the retrieval tool-call experience.
answeredQuestions.decision-enumerate-flexible-extensibilit: The PRD should explicitly enumerate the six flexible extensibility surfaces from the research — feedback, metadata, plugins[].data, tracePayload, contextEntries[].value, and toolCalls[].response — with documented default fallback behavior and plugin override points.
answeredQuestions.decision-reference-persistence-scoped-to: RAG-selected references should be persisted as plugin-owned data keyed to the specific retrieval tool call instance so selections remain tied to the exact call and turn that produced them.
answeredQuestions.decision-derived-item-level-retrieval-su: The plugin model should support derived item-level summaries from per-tool-call retrieval data for explorer, queue, and approval views, so those surfaces can use selected-reference counts, unreviewed retrieval states, and similar signals without flattening the stored data model.
answeredQuestions.decision-common-candidate-reference-cont: The PRD should require retrieval adapters to normalize different search engine outputs into a common candidate-reference contract for the retrieval review component, while preserving the raw backend or tool response for fallback rendering and auditability.
answeredQuestions.decision-runtime-fallback-for-plugin-com: The PRD should explicitly require plugin-contributed retrieval review components and other pluggable field components to run behind error boundaries with explicit fallback behavior so runtime failures degrade safely instead of breaking the workspace.
answeredQuestions.decision-lazy-loaded-low-overhead-extens: The PRD should explicitly require custom pluggable review and rendering components to be lazy-loaded and near-zero overhead when unused so optional RAG-specific extensibility does not materially increase generic host startup or bundle cost.

referencesProcessed.keyFindings: Defines the target agentic-first workspace: queue sidebar, split pane, tool-call-first review, evidence drawer, and lifecycle actions.
Defines the generic agentic core record and flexible extension surfaces such as feedback, metadata, plugins, context entries, tool calls, and trace payload.
Clarifies that tool calls are first-class, grounding summary/evaluation criteria should be removed from the host flow, feedback should be generic, and at least one required tool should be selected.
Captures the superseded separate-mode direction and provides historical context for what this new PRD is replacing.
conflicts: This new PRD replaces the separate-mode architecture with a single redesigned generic core.

nextActions: Validate the updated PRD language against the approved extensibility decisions and the RAG-as-tool-call mental model.
Translate the approved extensibility decisions into an implementation plan.
Review whether the superseded PRD needs any explicit cross-reference updates.
Confirm final approval language and ownership fields before PRD sign-off.
